Home › Itineraries › 3 days in Berlin
3 Days First-time visitors April through October

3 days in Berlin

The classic first-timer itinerary: landmarks in Mitte, Wall history in Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg, Charlottenburg and Tiergarten on day three.

Day 1

Mitte: landmarks back-to-back

Brandenburg Gate, Reichstag, Museum Island — Berlin's must-sees within walking distance.

  1. ☀️
    Morning
    9:00 – 10:30

    Start at Brandenburg Gate

    Pariser Platz, Adlon, Holocaust Memorial right next door. First 30 minutes to settle in.

    📍 Brandenburg Gate 🏙️ Mitte
    💡 Tip: Café 'Einstein Unter den Linden' is a 5-minute walk away.
  2. ☀️
    Morning
    10:30 – 12:00

    Reichstag with dome registration

    Foster's glass dome, free entry — but pre-registration a few days ahead is mandatory.

    📍 Reichstag
    💡 Tip: Online registration via bundestag.de, at least 1 week ahead.
  3. 🍽️
    Lunch
    12:30 – 14:00

    Lunch on Gendarmenmarkt

    Berlin's most beautiful square with concert hall and the two cathedrals. Cafés all around.

    📍 Gendarmenmarkt
  4. 🚶
    Afternoon
    14:00 – 18:00

    Museum Island

    Pergamon (partly closed), Neues Museum with Nefertiti, Bode Museum. One ticket for all 5 houses.

    📍 Museum Island
  5. 🌆
    Evening
    18:00 – 19:00

    TV Tower at sunset

    203m above Berlin. Pre-booking saves queue time.

    📍 TV Tower Alexanderplatz
  6. 🍷
    Dinner
    20:00

    Dinner at Hackescher Markt

    Lively restaurant scene with modern Berlin cuisine.

    🏙️ Mitte
Day 2

Wall history & Kreuzberg vibes

East Side Gallery, Checkpoint Charlie, Görli — Berlin's history from 1961 to today, on foot.

  1. ☀️
    Morning
    9:30 – 11:30

    East Side Gallery

    1.3km of Wall remnants with original murals, right along the Spree. Best light in the morning.

    📍 East Side Gallery 🏙️ Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg
  2. 🍽️
    Lunch
    12:00 – 13:00

    Markthalle Neun

    Thursdays: Street Food Thursday — Berlin classics & international stalls.

    🏙️ Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg
  3. 🚶
    Afternoon
    14:00 – 15:30

    Checkpoint Charlie + Wall Museum

    Most famous former crossing point, plus the Haus am Checkpoint Charlie (Wall escapes).

    📍 Checkpoint Charlie
  4. 🚶
    Afternoon
    16:00 – 18:00

    Walk through Kreuzberg 36

    Bergmannstraße, Görlitzer Park, Oranienstraße. Berlin's most Turkish and most alternative pocket at once.

    🏙️ Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg
  5. 🍷
    Dinner
    20:00

    Dinner in Kreuzberg

    From Berlin currywurst to Michelin-starred — Kreuzberg delivers in every price bracket.

Day 3

West Berlin & Tiergarten

Charlottenburg Palace, Kurfürstendamm, KaDeWe — the west side with the memorial church and Tiergarten walk.

  1. ☀️
    Morning
    9:30 – 11:30

    Charlottenburg Palace

    Berlin's largest Prussian palace, with garden for a stroll.

    📍 Charlottenburg Palace 🏙️ Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf
  2. 🍽️
    Lunch
    12:30 – 14:00

    KaDeWe gourmet floor

    6th floor of KaDeWe — sushi bars, oysters, pasta, Berlin meatballs. Big-city window.

    📍 KaDeWe
  3. 🚶
    Afternoon
    14:30 – 16:00

    Kurfürstendamm + memorial church

    Berlin's window-shopping boulevard, 3.5km long. The bombed-out Kaiser Wilhelm Memorial Church anchors it.

    📍 Kurfürstendamm
  4. 🚶
    Afternoon
    16:30 – 18:00

    Tiergarten walk

    Berlin's green centre, from Zoo Station towards Brandenburg Gate — 4km, around 50 min.

    📍 Tiergarten
  5. 🍷
    Dinner
    20:00

    Farewell dinner in Mitte or Ku'damm

    Depending on where the hotel is — both areas have options for the last night.

Where to stay

Hotels in the right neighborhoods

★★★★★
Mitte

Hotel Adlon Kempinski

The grande dame of Berlin hotels, right on Brandenburg Gate.

from €480/night
★★★★★
Mitte

Hotel de Rome

Rocco Forte's Berlin flagship on Bebelplatz, in the former Dresdner Bank.

from €420/night
★★★★★
Mitte

Regent Berlin

Five-star property on Gendarmenmarkt with the Charlotte & Fritz restaurant.

from €380/night

More itineraries

Other itineraries

2 Days · Families with kids (ages 4-12)

Berlin with kids

Family weekend in Berlin: Zoo, Tempelhof Field, Mauerpark flea market and the Natural History Museum — kid-friendly, with breaks.
1 Day · Anyone caught in the rain

Berlin when it rains

A rainy day in Berlin: three museums on Museum Island, lunch in the market hall, evening at KaDeWe or Sony Center.
